constraints: Preserve reconstructive React/CSS output and avoid image-based shortcuts.
Keep Storybook and example parity through shared source paths rather than forks.
Improve dark mode and accessibility-sensitive states without flattening the visual system.
Prefer shared reusable code/editor/diff and mockup controls over page-local wrappers.
gaps: Several visually important surfaces were recently extracted into primitives, but the broad convergence goal still required the example-app controls and docs code figure to move into shared kit APIs rather than staying app/page-local.

Dark-mode and accessibility quality are generally improved, but most hierarchy still depends on shared CSS vocabulary rather than deeply prop-driven variants, so each newly extracted surface must preserve those states carefully.
Storybook coverage needed to expose shared controls and newly extracted motifs directly, not only through composed page previews.
reusableSurfaces: Seraph primitives including specimen and ledger variants
Codex docs/dashboard primitives including docs code figures
specAnchors: shared code surface source in src/components/Code

instructions: Refactor and improve the kit to match specs and improve reusability across use cases. Componentize more of the mock-specific structures, converge visuals toward the local mocks, improve dark mode, fix accessibility issues, deepen Storybook controls and example parity, and iterate until completion.
Operate directly on repository files. Do not only describe changes.
Prioritize shared primitives, configurable sections/pages, and reusable styling vocabulary over page-local patches.
Push fidelity and componentization together: if a surface is visually important and still local, extract it.
Improve Storybook controls/motifs and keep example parity through the same source paths.
Fix dark-mode and accessibility-sensitive states where touched.
Prefer reusable code/editor/diff adoption in docs/chat/mock surfaces where appropriate.
prompt.task: Implement the highest-value broad kit convergence work directly in the repository.
